# Platform Independence Strategy

Do not let one platform own the strategy.

## Platform-specific risk

```text
TikTok: personalised feed volatility, trend decay, eligibility limits
YouTube Shorts: view metric inflation after play/replay counting, need engaged metrics
Instagram Reels: follower/non-follower split, social graph effects
LinkedIn/X: topic and network dependence
```

## Independence rules

1. Every core mechanism must have platform-native translations.
2. Every winning video must become at least one evergreen/search asset.
3. Every batch must produce one asset that can live outside the feed:
   - blog post
   - newsletter snippet
   - checklist
   - carousel
   - long-form video segment
   - pinned profile explainer
4. Avoid building only trend-dependent assets.

## Platform translation table

| Mechanism | TikTok | Reels | Shorts |
|---|---|---|---|
| First-frame contrast | culture/search hook | social/send framing | thumbnail-like clarity |
| Caption checklist | Creator Search Insights topic | save/send CTA | direct search title |
| Retention teardown | comment-driven reply | Trial Reel test | engaged-view analysis |
| Myth-bust | conversational contrarian | shareable identity | title-led proof |

## Why this matters

A mechanism that only works under one platform’s current surface rules is fragile. A mechanism that works after translation is closer to a true behavioural pattern.
